quoteFirst: He informs me that for ten years past he has been obliged, in order to procure sleep, to take ten grains on retiring, and ten grains more
quoteLast: His health is otherwise good, and he is possessed of more than common intellectual power.
title: Drugs that enslave
author: Kane, H. H. (Harry Hubbell), 1854-1906

quoteLast: we found, in a Fiumara, some holes which supplied us with bitter water, truly delicious after fifteen hours of thirst.

author: Daudet, Alphonse, 1840-1897

quoteFirst: “Well I may,” was the reply; “I dined with the Guards’ Mess yesterday, and went knocking about with Bellingham and Annesley afterwards; got home
quoteLast: , had a cigar and a bottle of soda-water, changed my dress clothes, and slept in the arm-chair until Tirrett came for me in a dog-cart at half-past four,--for they take the Don out as soon as its light.”
title: Harry Coverdale's Courtship, and All That Came of It
author: Smedley, Frank E. (Frank Edward), 1818-1864
